Q17 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Principle of minimum strain energy is
1. applicable when the redundant support yields.
2. applicable only when the redundant support does not yield.
3. a particular case of Castigliano’s second theorem.
4. a particular case of Castigliano’s first theorem.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Principle of minimum strain energy is a special case of Castigliano’s first theorem and is applicable
only when redundant support does not yield.

Q20 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding approximate analysis of statically indeterminate structures:
1. The portal method is most suitable for buildings having low elevation and uniform framing.
2. In portal method, the shear at the exterior column hinges is twice that at interior column hinges.
3. In cantilever method, the axial stress in a column is proportional to its distance from the centroid of the cross-sectional areas of the columns at a given floor level.
Which of the above statement are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
In portal method, the shear at interior column hinges is twice that at exterior column hinges. The
rational behind it is that the interior columns would represent the effect of two portal columns
and would therefore carry twice the shear as the two exterior columns.

Match List-I (Type of timber) with List-II (Seasoning characteristics) and select the correct answer using the codes given below the lists:

 
Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Timbers are classified on the basis of seasoning characteristics (cracking and splitting during
normal air-seasoning practice) into three categories:
• Highly refractory (Class A)
• Moderately refractory (Class B)
• Non-refractory (Class C)

Q25 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following constituents of brick earth, when present in excess, change the colour of the brick from red to yellow?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Lime in brick clay aids in following:
• Reduces shrinkage on drying
• Acts as flux
• Lowers the fusion temperature
When it is present in excess:
• Causes the brick to melt
• Change the colour of bricks from red to yellow.

Q29 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements regarding the use of fly ash in concrete are correct?
1. Fly ash reduces the permeability of concrete.
2. Fly ash enhances the resistance of concrete to sulphate attack.
3. Fly ash increases the heat of hydration of concrete.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• Fly ash fills voids in concrete → reduces permeability.
• It improves resistance against sulphate attack (better durability).
• It actually reduces the heat of hydration, not increases.

Q30 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

The major chemical constituents of fly ash are:

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Fly ash is mainly composed of silica, alumina, and ferric oxide, which make it a good pozzolanic
material used in cement concrete.

Q42 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Equilibrium condition, yield condition (M ≤ Mp) and mechanism condition (formation of a plastic collapse mechanism) are the conditions to be satisfied by any correct plastic analysis results.
Which of the above condition does the statical methods of plastic analysis consider?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Conditions used for static method of plastic analysis
(i) Equilibrium condition
(ii) Yield condition
Conditions used for kinematic method of plastic analysis
(i) Equilibrium condition
(ii) Mechanism condition

Q43 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

An ordinary mild steel bar has been prestressed to a working stress of 210 MPa. Young's modulus of steel is 200 GPa. Permanent negative strain due to shrinkage and creep is 0.0008. The effective stress left in steel is

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Loss due to shrinkage and creep = 200 × 103 × 0.0008 = 160 MPa
∴ Effective stress left in steel = 210 – 160 = 50 MPa

Q46 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statements are correct regarding a doubly reinforced beam?
1. It reduces short-term deflection due to shrinkage.
2. It can provide resistance in seismic zone to withstand repeated reversals of loading
3. It is provided when depth of beam is restricted and applied bending moment exceeds the capacity of a singly reinforced beam.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Doubly reinforced section is designed when:
• Depth of beam is restricted.
• High bending moment is acting on beam.
• Reversal of stress is there.
• Ductility requirement is there.

Q60 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following statement(s) is/are correct?
1. Free float for an activity is its earliest finish time minus earliest start time.
2. Total float is the difference between maximum time available and actual time required for the completion of the activity.
3. Independent float is excess of minimum available time over the required activity duration.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Free float is earliest start time of its successor activity minus earliest finish time of activity.

where QP = Maximum flood discharge (in m3/s), CR = Ryve’s coefficient, CD = Dicken’s constant

Correct answer: A

(a)

Q86 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
(I) The 'Primary treatment' part involves screening, grit removal and settling so that the settleable and floating solids are removed and giving about 30-35% reduction in biochemical oxygen demand.
(II) The 'Secondary treatment' generally consists of a biological step in which the dissolved organic matter is converted into a settleable form and removed as sludge by settling in secondary sedimentation tank.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Primary treatment is often called clarification sedimentation. Objective of primary treatment of
wastewater is to produce a liquid effluent of suitably improved quality for treatment stage i.e.
secondary biological treatment.

Q89 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In high rate trickling filter eon share filtration adopted mainly when
1. Influent BOD is large
2. Effluent BOD desired is less than 30 mg/l
3. Removal of nitrogenous matter
4. Removal of suspended solid
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Nitrogenous matter may not get sufficient time for nitrification, as the rate of flow is large in high
rate trickling filter, Biological film may get sloughed before nitrification has the time to take place
and so 2nd stage filtration may be adopted with carbonaceous matter getting removed in 1st stage
and nitrification in 2nd stage of filteration.

Correct answer: C

(c)
Nitrates in waste water is removed by ion exchange, reverse osmosis and distillation process.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

beneath pile is more in pile group. However settlement of pile can be even greater than pile group.
Under reamed pile is designed for both point bearing resistance and skin friction resistance.

Correct answer: D
Solution

sowing different crops together in the same field. Mixed cropping is not desirable as different
crops require different amount of water and field conditions. Farmers are not benefitted. If
irrigation water is assured, mixed cropping may be eliminated and single superior crop may be
grown to get the maximum benefits.

Q115 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Fly levelling is generally used for the reconnaissance of the area.
2. Profile levelling is done for the purpose of determining the elevation of ground surface along a fixed line.
3. Hypsometric levelling is not dependent on change in the atmospheric pressure.
Which of the above statements is/are incorrect?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Fly levelling is used for reconnaissance surveys and to check approximate levels where
speed is prioritized over high precision.
• Profile levelling is indeed done to determine ground elevations along a fixed line, typically
for linear infrastructure projects.
• Hypsometric levelling is based on the principle that the boiling point of water changes with
atmospheric pressure, which in turn varies with altitude. Therefore, it depends on the change
in atmospheric pressure.

Correct answer: C

makes with the horizontal line.

Correct answer: C
Solution

5. McLeod method
6. Burmister method

The percentage of time in a year during which cross wind component remains within the limit is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Creep is defined as the longitudinal movement of the rails along the track relative to the sleepers.

Q131 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which one of the following statements is not correct for heading and benching method of tunneling?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The heading and benching method involves excavating the top portion (heading) first, followed
by the bottom portion (benching). This creates a confined space in the heading, making the removal
of muck more challenging than in a full-face excavation.

Q133 Not checked

Each of the next Eighteen (18) items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): The elements of the leading diagonal of the stiffness matrix are always positive, while the other elements may be positive or negative.
Statement (II): The displacement along any coordinate due to unit force at that coordinate is always in the direction of the applied unit force.

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Diagonal elements of both flexibility as well as stiffness matrix are always positive.
This is because displacement at any coordinate due to unit load at that coordinate is always in the
same direction of unit load.

Q135 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next Eighteen (18) items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Vegetation and forest cover reduce the peak flow of hydrograph.
Statement (II): Vegetation and forest cover reduces the infiltration

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Vegetation and forest cover increases the infiltration and storage capacities of the soil. Further
they cause considerable reduction in the overland flow.
• Vegetation cover slows down rain drop impact, reducing surface scaling and soil compaction.
• Roots create pores and improve soil structure allowing more water to percolate into the ground.

Statement (II): The value of adhesion coefficient (α) is smaller in stiff or over consolidated clays.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Even though the value of α is smaller in stiff or over consolidated clays, the skin friction resistance
is higher because of much higher value of cohesion, Cu.

Q140 Not checked

(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Pre chlorination is done in water source, which are highly turbid and highly polluted.
Statement (II): Turbidity tends to protect the micro organisms present in water from disinfectants.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Pre chlorination is not done in highly turbid water because turbidity tends to shield the micro
organism from disinfectant and also tends to carry some of the disinfectant on it self, thereby
wasting the whole purpose of pre chlorination.

Q141 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next Eighteen (18) items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (II): Imhoff tank is not economical due to requirement of skilled attention during operation.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Imhoff tank when used in small treatment plant requires only primary treatment. It is quite
economical and does not require skilled attention during operation.

(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is not the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): In under reinforced section at limiting stage, steel yields first and elongate significantly without any increase in stress.
Statement (II): The marked increase in tensile strain, causes the neutral axis to shift downwards.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
The marked increase in tensile strain causes the NA to shift upwards, and thus tends to reduce the area of concrete under compression to maintain total compressive force equal to total tensile force.
